source_pr.title: expression: fix extract bug when argument is a negative duration
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: guo-shaoge <shaoge1994@163.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #27236 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: `extract` didn't handle situation when its argument is a negative duration.

### What is changed and how it works?


How it Works:

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [x] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

```release-note
expression: fix extract bug when argument is a negative duration
```

symptoms: row = tk.MustQuery(`select extract(hour_second from c1) from t order by c1;`) (expression/integration_test.go)
failure_signature: row = tk.MustQuery(`select extract(hour_second from c1) from t order by c1;`) (expression/integration_test.go)
